description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: build, selenium, and deploy. In the build stage add a create-html job that uses the image registry.gitlab.com/oer/emacs-reveal/emacs-reveal:9.17.4, runs before_script git submodule sync --recursive and git submodule update --init --recursive, then executes emacs --batch --load elisp/publish.el, find public -type f -regex '.*\\.(html|js|css)$' -exec gzip -f -k {} \\; , mkdir public/pdfs, chmod 777 public/pdfs, and find \"$(pwd -P)/public\" -type f -regex '.*/Operating-Systems-[A-Za-z-]+\\.html' > public/pdfs/urls.txt; it stores the public directory as an artifact (expire in 4 weeks) and runs only on the master branch. In the selenium stage add a generate-pdf job that uses the image registry.gitlab.com/oer/emacs-reveal/selenium-chrome, provides the selenium/standalone-chrome service, and runs python3 \"/root/selenium-print-pdf.py\" \"$(pwd -P)/public/pdfs/urls.txt\" selenium__standalone-chrome \"$(pwd -P)/public/pdfs\"; it also archives the public directory as an artifact (expire in 4 weeks) and runs only on master. In the deploy stage add a pages job that runs on the master branch, executes a dummy echo command, and publishes the public directory as an artifact (expire in 4 weeks) for GitLab Pages.
